title: Separate Salesforce Description from User-Defined Definition in Data Dictionary
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see both the Salesforce field description and my own custom definition in the data dictionary, so that I can reference the official metadata alongside my team's documentation.
description:
Distinguish between the Salesforce-sourced field description and user-entered definition in the data dictionary.

### Desired UX
- **Salesforce Description** column: pulled from Salesforce metadata (read-only)
- **Definition** column: user-editable field for custom documentation
- **Merged tooltip**: hovering shows both description and definition. Clicking opens a modal with editable definition text area

### CodePen Mockup
A prototype was created demonstrating the merged tooltip with click-to-edit behavior.

### Technical Notes
- Salesforce `description` field may require specific permissions to fetch
- `HelpText` field is available as an alternative if description cannot be fetched
acceptance_criteria: Salesforce description is pulled and stored separately from user-entered definition
User can edit the definition but not the Salesforce description
Tooltip shows merged view of both fields
Click on tooltip opens modal for editing definition
ux_changes: Merged tooltip showing Salesforce description + user definition. Click-to-edit modal for definition field.
middleware_changes: Fetch Salesforce description field from metadata API. Store separately from user-defined definition.
data_model_changes: Separate columns for Salesforce description (read-only) and user definition (editable) in object_fields table.
implementation_notes: Waqar was able to fetch HelpText but not description from Salesforce — may be a permissions issue. Definition is currently editable via the edit icon on row selection.
comment_summary: Waqar clarified that definition wasn't coming from Salesforce and asked which field to use. He found HelpText was available but the description field wasn't accessible, possibly due to permissions.
